blocks.content: After practice, Coach called it. I was packing up my bag at the sideline when I heard Jonathan.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Hey — you writing something about the team?
I looked up. He was standing at the bottom of the bleachers, not quite climbing them, just — calling up. She looked down from her notebook.

blocks.speaker: Stacy
blocks.content: No. I'm writing about how practice works. The rhythms of it.

blocks.speaker: Stacy
blocks.content: Yeah. Like — when a play clicks and when it doesn't. What changes in the body language. It's for a writing class.
Jonathan looked at me. I raised my eyebrows exactly once. He ignored me.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: What was your grade on the last one?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: You can probably get the A on this one. Today was a good practice.
She smiled. Not like she was trying to — like she couldn't help it.
I stood at the sideline with my bag on my shoulder and watched Jonathan do exactly what he told me not to do. The difference was — he wasn't scared. Not even a little.
